test_suite.details: node test.js — 3,518 passed, 0 failed (Sections 1–9 cover wins.json schema, HTML output consistency, internal links, build pipeline, data cross-references, sections.json schema, prose content integrity, prediction panels, Phase A design refresh).

internal_anchors.details: 12 ds-tab-content tabs (overview, evaluate, model, selftest, wins, pages, killshots, timestamp, predictions, falsify, ai, refs); 12 nav-bar showTab buttons all map to existing tabs; 208 distinct href="#…" anchors all resolve to id= targets in the document; all 71 wins.json IDs (including 057b/058b sub-entries) have matching id="winNNN" detail anchors. Tab anchors are also fully covered by test.js Section 3.

tab_structure.details: 12 tabs, 0 orphans, 0 nav buttons pointing to nonexistent tabs, default-active = overview (`<div class="ds-tab-content active" id="overview">`). The codebase no longer uses sectionNav() prev/next chain — that helper was retired in favor of the tab-bar nav model.

heading_hierarchy.details: 0 skipped heading levels detected, no h2 before h1 within any tab.

broken: https://john09289.github.io/predictions/raw-text/03-predictions.txt → 404 on two attempts (dome-side link rot — author removed the /raw-text/ mirror path; referenced once in data/sections.json part4 ~ 9-station eclipse table). Carry-forward from 2026-05-01.
timeouts: https://podaac.jpl.nasa.gov/ → 000 (TCP connection timed out from sandbox after 12s on retry; canonical PO.DAAC root, reachable in browsers)
Note: https://www.chime-frb.ca and https://funwithscience.net/dome-model-review/ which timed out yesterday are reachable (200) on this run, confirming those were transient sandbox-side artifacts.
external_links.details: 85 non-citation data-source URLs checked (citation/DOI/publisher links deferred to curmudgeon per prompt). 81/85 returned 200 directly. 1 genuine 404 (dome-side link rot — same /raw-text/ mirror as yesterday). 3 podaac.jpl.nasa.gov URLs return 000 (TCP timeouts) — sandbox-side artifact, real site is alive. NOAA URLs returned 200 with our requested User-Agent (no bot-blocking 403 this run).

data_prose_consistency.details: wins.json length=71 (69 dome WIN numbers + 057b/058b paired sub-entries). Verdict tallies in HTML render exactly match wins.json: Refuted by Data 9, Self-Contradicted 14, Misleading 22, Std Model Explains 18, Not Demonstrated 4, Unfalsifiable 4 (sum=71). 0 unresolved {{...}} placeholders in docs/index.html. uncounted-failures.json count=10. code_analysis flags: reviewed=70, relabels_standard=50, post_hoc=69, derives_from_dome=3.

win_detail_consistency.details: All 71 wins.json IDs have a matching id="winNNN" detail anchor in docs/index.html (including win057b/win058b sub-entry anchors). All 71 summary-table rows present and carry the correct verdict CSS class (v-refuted / v-selfcon / v-misleading / v-std / v-notdemo / v-unfalsifiable). All 71 WINs have non-empty tldr_evidence and tldr_verdict fields. 8 detail_groups all reference consistent group strings.

win_dome_alignment.details: Fetched john09289.github.io/predictions/wins.html (91,686 bytes). 69 unique WIN-NNN numbers (WIN-001 through WIN-069), 100% covered by our review. 8 PROS-NNN aliases (PROS-001..PROS-008) present. The 057b/058b sub-entries are documented in llms.txt and intentionally capture distinct claims that the dome assigned the same WIN number — not collisions on our side.

issues: 21 <h2> headings inside tabs are NOT wrapped in <details>/<summary class='ps-summary|ks-summary'>. Same set as 2026-05-01: overview (6), model (1), wins (7 — including '3.1 Verdict Summary Table' through '3.6 Detailed: Misleading and Unfalsifiable'), pages (1), killshots (2), timestamp (1), predictions (2). Many legitimately render leading section headers for tables/scorecards where progressive disclosure would be intrusive — but the wins-tab '3.x Detailed: ...' subsection headers remain candidates for ps-summary wrapping.
progressive_disclosure.details: WIN-level disclosure (Evidence/Verdict ks-summary/ks-tldr) is fully consistent across all 71 WINs. No regression vs prior runs.

issues: moderate: 4 hardcoded pastel backgrounds in sections.json part5.html (background:#FFE0B2 ×2, background:#BBDEFB ×2) — color-coded comparison cells, likely intentional but should migrate to utility classes
minor: 14 hardcoded #ddd border colors in sections.json part5.html — all on data-table tr style='border-bottom:1px solid #ddd;'; should use var(--border)
minor: 3 hardcoded grey text colors color:#555 in part4.html, part5.html, part6.html — should use var(--muted) or similar
hardcoded_theme_colors.details: 0 #fff / #ffffff / #f8f9fa / white-named light backgrounds (which would actually break the dark theme). Same set of stylistic-cleanliness findings as 2026-05-01; no regression.

discoverability.details: docs/llms.txt (5,595 B): contains 'funwithscience.net/dome-model-review' canonical URL, mentions ECM/dome model by name, '71 WIN entries documenting all 69 dome WIN numbers' matches wins.json length. docs/sitemap.xml: well-formed XML, 2 <loc> entries (main page + llms.txt). docs/robots.txt: 'Allow: /' + 'Sitemap: https://funwithscience.net/dome-model-review/sitemap.xml'. data/uncounted-failures.json: 10 entries, all FAIL-NNN, no duplicate IDs, all required schema fields present. docs/index.html <head> meta tags: <meta name='description'> present, og:title/og:description present, ClaimReview JSON-LD present.

issues: moderate: 1 ID gap — EXP-052 has no tracker entry and no expansions/EXP-052*.json file. Same isolated gap as 2026-05-01. Looks like a skipped allocation rather than lost work.
expansion_tracker.details: items=282, next_id=284, max_id=283 → next_id > max_id, no collision risk. 0 phantom entries (status=complete with output_file pointing to a missing file). 0 stale pending entries (>48h old). 0 orphaned files in monitor/analyst/expansions/.

issues: minor: monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/WIN-067.c3.json.build.js (0 bytes, tab-completion artifact, carry-forward from 2026-04-21)
minor: monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-202.json.bak (21,964 B backup, canonical EXP-202.json present in both workspace and git, carry-forward)
workspace_only_files.details: Diff between workspace and a fresh git clone: curmudgeon/reviews 405 (workspace) vs 404 (git) — the +1 is the 0-byte tab-completion artifact; analyst/expansions 192 vs 191 — the +1 is the .bak file. No fresh agent-output JSONs are workspace-only. The MAJOR finding from 2026-05-01 (WIN-026.c3.json) has been rescued by workspace-sync.

priority_queue_coupling.details: 0 section-new or category-proposal-writeup tracker items missing from monitor/curmudgeon/priority-queue.json (0 such non-integrated items currently exist).

workspace_sync_skip_log.details: 0 direction-violation skips and 133 mtime-guard skips in the last 24h (1,125 historical lines in workspace-sync-skips.jsonl). Mtime-guard skips are normal operation (decider committing workspace-owned files directly to git). No writers on the wrong side of the boundary.

issues: MAJOR-borderline: 14 unprocessed reviews (just over the >10 'systematic gap' threshold). However, prefix breakdown is mixed — EXP×4, HNOTE×1, INTEGRITY×1, PRED×1, SEC×3, WIN×4 — so this is decider backlog, not a digest filter regression. Oldest unprocessed: SEC-4.2.7a.c1.json (2026-04-28 22:04Z, ~3.4 days), WIN-044.c5.json (2026-04-28 14:05Z, ~3.7 days), EXP-259-SEC1.8-WIN016.c1.json (2026-04-29 14:05Z, ~2.7 days). Most recent: EXP-283-proposal.json and HNOTE-OPERATOR-FROZEN-PRED-REVIEW-RUBRIC-001-consolidation.json from this morning. The decider should sweep these on its next run.
digest_coverage.details: Total review files=447; unique base IDs (latest cycle)=247; processed ledger=428; unprocessed=14. Mixed prefix distribution rules out a categorical digest-filter regression. Treating as MODERATE backlog (not MAJOR systematic gap) because the spread of prefixes argues against a filter bug.

broken_review_files.details: Mandatory digest regeneration ran in the clean clone: digest-reviews.js exit=0; pending-digest.json errors[]=[]. 0 parse failures.

claude_md_mechanical.details: All 8 expected scheduled-agent rows present in CLAUDE.md schedule table (poller, analyst, curmudgeon, decider, integrity, tinker, social, workspace-sync). All 25 spot-checked file paths exist on disk. 0 hardcoded numerical-count phrases of the form '<number> WINs/tests/issues/claims/entries/pending' found in CLAUDE.md prose.

build_reproducibility.details:
Cloned funwithscience-org/dome-model-review into /tmp/dome-integrity-clone, ran `node build.js html`. diff vs published docs/index.html: 4 lines, all on the dateline `

May 1, 2026 …` → `May 2, 2026 …`. The build is byte-reproducible from the source data; the only diff is the date stamp the build picks up at run time. No data drift.

issues_found.description: 14 curmudgeon reviews remain unprocessed by the decider, just over the >10 'systematic gap' threshold. Prefix breakdown is mixed (EXP×4, HNOTE×1, INTEGRITY×1, PRED×1, SEC×3, WIN×4), which rules out a categorical digest-filter regression — this is decider backlog. Oldest unprocessed are 3.4–3.7 days old (SEC-4.2.7a.c1.json from 2026-04-28, WIN-044.c5.json from 2026-04-28, EXP-259-SEC1.8-WIN016.c1.json from 2026-04-29).
issues_found.suggested_fix: Run the decider to drain the queue. If unprocessed count is still >10 after the next decider run, escalate to MAJOR — that would suggest the decider is choking on a specific review (e.g., the INTEGRITY-EXP-283-MISSING-2026-05-01.json or HNOTE-OPERATOR-FROZEN-PRED-REVIEW-RUBRIC-001 entries) rather than just being behind.

issues_found.description: External data-source link returns 404: https://john09289.github.io/predictions/raw-text/03-predictions.txt — referenced once in sections.json (part4, eclipse 9-station prose) as a 'raw text mirror' of the dome's predictions page. The dome author appears to have removed the /raw-text/ path on his site. Carry-forward from 2026-05-01.
issues_found.location: data/sections.json (part4.html, eclipse 9-station table prose) → rendered into docs/index.html ×1
issues_found.suggested_fix: Either swap the link to a still-resolving dome URL (e.g. https://john09289.github.io/predictions/predictions.html) or drop the inline link and keep only the table content; our prose stands on its own without the upstream raw-text mirror.

issues_found.description: expansion-tracker.json has a 1-id gap at EXP-052 — no tracker entry and no expansions/EXP-052*.json file. next_id invariant still holds (284 > 283). Same gap as 2026-05-01; appears to be a skipped allocation rather than lost work.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Optional — analyst can git-blame the allocation date to confirm this was a writer-side abandon and not a silently-dropped item.

issues_found.description: 21 <h2> headings inside tabs are not wrapped in <details>/<summary class='ps-summary|ks-summary'>. Same set as 2026-05-01. The wins-tab '3.x Detailed: ...' subsection headers in particular look like they should follow the disclosure pattern; killshots, timestamp, and predictions tabs each have one or two unwrapped section headers.
issues_found.location: docs/index.html (wins, killshots, timestamp, predictions, model, pages, overview tabs)
issues_found.suggested_fix: Decider/analyst pass — wrap 3.2/3.3/3.4/3.5/3.6 detailed-verdict headers in details/summary; treat overview scorecard and table-leading headers as legitimate exceptions.

issues_found.description: 4 hardcoded pastel backgrounds in sections.json part5.html (background:#FFE0B2 ×2, background:#BBDEFB ×2) on color-coded comparison cells. Likely intentional but should be utility classes (e.g., .data-table-warning / .data-table-info) so future theme changes do not need to chase inline styles.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Promote .data-table-warning / .data-table-info utility classes in the stylesheet and replace the inline styles.

issues_found.description: 14 hardcoded #ddd table borders and 3 hardcoded #555 grey text colors in sections.json part4/5/6.html. No actual light-theme breakage (no #fff or background:white); stylistic-cleanliness only.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Migrate the #ddd borders to var(--border) and the #555 text to var(--muted) or similar.

issues_found.description: 32 WINs have new_in_v51=true in wins.json, but the rendered HTML does not put a per-row marker (asterisk / NEW badge / ★) on those summary-table rows. The build does use {{NEW_IN_V51}} as an aggregate count placeholder in prose (correctly resolved to 32), so the data IS reflected — just not at the row level. Carry-forward from 2026-05-01; integrity spec Section 5 expects per-row markers.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Either (a) add a small NEW badge / asterisk to summary-row rendering for new_in_v51 entries, or (b) update the integrity prompt's Section 5 description to drop the per-row asterisk requirement (the aggregate count in prose is sufficient signal). Either resolution is fine; choose one.

issues_found.description: podaac.jpl.nasa.gov and podaac-tools.jpl.nasa.gov return 000 (TCP connection timeouts) from this sandbox after 12s. Likely a sandbox-side networking artifact for podaac specifically — yesterday's www.chime-frb.ca and funwithscience.net timeouts cleared on this run. Sites are reachable in browsers.
issues_found.suggested_fix: No action — sandbox-side TCP timeout on a real site is not a broken link. Re-check next run; if still timing out only podaac, file as known-flaky.

issues_found.description: Two harmless workspace-only artifacts persist (carry-forward): monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/WIN-067.c3.json.build.js (0 bytes, accidental tab-completion 2026-04-21) and monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-202.json.bak (historical backup, canonical EXP-202.json present in both workspace and git).
issues_found.suggested_fix: Delete both manually next session. Both are inert — neither participates in any pipeline.

summary: Overall WARN. The published site is structurally sound: build is reproducible from source data (only diff is the date stamp picking up today's date), test suite green (3,518/3,518), all 12 tabs reachable, all 71 WIN detail panels intact, verdict tallies match wins.json exactly (9+14+22+18+4+4=71), full coverage of the dome's 69 WIN numbers, llms.txt/sitemap.xml/robots.txt/ClaimReview JSON-LD all valid, 0 parse errors in curmudgeon reviews after mandatory digest regeneration, 0 priority-queue decoupling, 0 workspace-sync direction violations. Yesterday's MAJOR (workspace-only WIN-026.c3.json) has been rescued by workspace-sync. Today's most notable finding is digest-coverage backlog: 14 unprocessed curmudgeon reviews (>10 threshold), but the mixed prefix distribution rules out a digest-filter regression — this is decider backlog with the oldest items 3.4–3.7 days old; classified MODERATE. Other moderates are carry-forward: dome-side raw-text mirror 404, EXP-052 tracker gap, 21 unwrapped h2s, pastel inline backgrounds in part5. Minors are stylistic, sandbox-side podaac timeouts, and the new_in_v51 per-row marker mismatch. No critical findings, no build drift, no bypassed adversarial review.
